title: Creating Strong Passwords
drDigitalIntro:
A strong password is your first line of defense.
• Make it long — at least 12 characters.
• Mix uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.
• Avoid your name or birthday, and use a different one for each account.

steps.say: Type a password and make it strong — use 12+ characters with uppercase, numbers, and symbols.

drDigitalSuccess: You built a strong password! The meter checks length, uppercase, numbers, and symbols — use all four for the best protection.
drDigitalHint: Try mixing uppercase letters, numbers, and symbols like !@# to make it stronger. Aim for 12+ characters.
